title: Eid day
description: A quiet coastal study of morning light, working harbors, and the stillness that arrives before a city fully wakes.

cover.alt: A cinematic coastal landscape at dusk
cover.caption: First light across the coast.

images.alt: Wide coastal landscape with soft light
images.caption: The shoreline before the streets become loud.

images.alt: A road cutting through a quiet landscape
images.caption: A road inland from the water.

images.alt: A mountain valley with layered atmosphere
images.caption: Distance held in soft contrast.

images.alt: Open water and trees under a muted sky
images.caption: Weather crossing the waterline.

title: Two Broken Nations
description:
The second-leg CAF Confederation Cup match between Somalia’s Dekedaha FC and Sudan’s Alzamala SC at Kasarani Stadium in Nairobi on September 27, 2025, was more than a football fixture. It brought together two clubs carrying the hopes of nations shaped by conflict, displacement, and decades of uncertainty. 
Neither team could host the match at home. Ongoing security concerns and limited sporting infrastructure in both Somalia and Sudan have forced their clubs to play continental competitions far from their own supporters. 
Yet, despite these challenges, the game stood as a reminder of the resilience that defines both countries. For many young Somalis and Sudanese, football remains one of the few spaces where dreams still feel possible. On dusty pitches, in crowded neighborhoods, and in communities marked by hardship, countless young people continue to imagine futures beyond war and instability.

title: City After Rain
description: Street scenes shaped by reflection, late buses, storefront light, and the brief theatre of weather passing through Nairobi.

cover.caption: Rain leaves a second city behind.

images.alt: A building facade with dramatic light
images.caption: Architecture sharpened by wet air.

images.alt: Angular building against a muted sky
images.caption: Hard lines, soft weather.

images.alt: A quiet tree-lined place after rain
images.caption: A pause between movements.

images.alt: Urban street at night with reflective surfaces
images.caption: Night folds into the road.

title: Portraits In Between
description: Portraits made in transitional spaces - doorways, courtyards, rehearsal rooms - where expression arrives unannounced.

cover.alt: Black and white portrait of a thoughtful man
cover.caption: On February 8, 2023, I visited Balidoogle Airfield, which was hosting an ongoing event for newly graduated Danab Cadets. At the time, I was serving as Chief Special Photographer to the Prime Minister of Somalia.

images.caption: President Duma Gideon Boko visits the BBC East Africa Bureau for an interview during his official visit to Kenya. Boko was inaugurated in November 2024 after his Umbrella for Democratic Change (UDC) won a landslide election, ending 58 years of rule by the Botswana Democratic Party (BDP).

images.alt: A close portrait of a woman looking toward camera
images.caption: A held gaze in window light.

images.alt: A portrait with soft studio light
images.caption: Quiet, direct, unforced.
The room becomes part of the face.

title: Desert Field Notes
description: A spare visual notebook of heat, road dust, long shadows, and the human scale of open land.

cover.alt: A cinematic road through open desert terrain
cover.caption: Road dust becomes atmosphere.

images.alt: A desert landscape under open sky
images.caption: A horizon that refuses to hurry.

images.alt: A solitary animal crossing open land
images.caption: Scale, distance, patience.
